Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Merge all big contours into one\n        all_points = np.vstack(big_contours)\n        x, y, w, h = cv2.boundingRect(all_points)\n        area = sum(cv2.contourArea(c) for c in big_contours)\n        \n        # Read first frame as background (no runner on track)\n    ret, bg_frame = cap.read()\n    if not ret:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot read first frame\")\n\n    bg_gray = cv2.cvtColor(bg_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n    bg_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(bg_gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n    frame_num = 0\n    frame_data = []  # (frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y)\n\n    while True:\n        ret, frame = cap.read()\n        if not ret:\n            break\n        frame_num += 1\n\n        gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n        gray_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n        # Background subtraction\n        diff = cv2.absdiff(bg_blur, gray_blur)\n        _, thresh = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)\n\n        # Morphological cleanup\n        kernel = cv2.getStructuringElement(cv2.MORPH_ELLIPSE, (5, 5))\n        thresh = cv2.morphologyEx(thresh, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)\n        thresh = cv2.dilate(thresh, kernel, iterations=2)\n\n        fg_pixels = np.where(thresh > 0)\n        fg_area = len(fg_pixels[0])\n\n        if fg_area > 300:\n            lowest_y = int(np.max(fg_pixels[0]))\n            frame_data.append((frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y))\n\n    cap.release()\n\n    if not frame_data:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No athlete detected in video\")\n\n    # Extract the main athlete sequence (largest continuous run of frames with\n    # significant foreground area, filtering out noise)\n    athlete_frames = extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data)\n\n    if len(athlete_frames) < 5:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Athlete sequence too short\")\n\n    # Determine ground level from running phases\n    frames_arr = np.array([d[0] for d in athlete_frames])\n    lowest_ys = np.array([d[2] for d in athlete_frames])\n\n    # Ground level = the typical maximum lowest_y during running\n    # Use the 90th percentile of lowest_y values as ground reference\n    ground_level = np.percentile(lowest_ys, 85)\n\n    # Find the jump peak (minimum lowest_y, i.e., feet highest off ground)\n    # Only consider frames with substantial area (athlete fully visible)\n    areas = np.array([d[1] for d in athlete_frames])\n    area_threshold = np.percentile(areas, 25)\n\n    significant_mask = areas >= area_threshold\n    significant_lowest_ys = lowest_ys.copy()\n    significant_lowest_ys[~significant_mask] = ground_level  # ignore low-area frames\n\n    peak_idx = np.argmin(significant_lowest_ys)\n    peak_lowest_y = significant_lowest_ys[peak_idx]\n\n    # The jump threshold: midpoint between ground and peak, biased toward ground\n    # This helps distinguish jumping from normal running stride variation\n    jump_threshold = ground_level - 0.25 * (ground_level - peak_lowest_y)\n\n    # Find takeoff: working backwards from peak, find the last frame where\n    # lowest_y is at or near ground level\n    takeoff_idx = peak_idx\n    for i in range(peak_idx - 1, -1, -1):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= jump_threshold:\n            takeoff_idx = i\n            break\n\n    # Find landing: working forwards from peak, find the first frame where\n    # lowest_y returns to near ground level\n    landing_idx = peak_idx\n    for i in range(peak_idx + 1, len(lowest_ys)):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= jump_threshold:\n            landing_idx = i\n            break\n\n    takeoff_frame = int(frames_arr[takeoff_idx])\n    landing_frame = int(frames_arr[landing_idx])\n\n    return takeoff_frame, landing_frame\n\n\ndef extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data):\n    \"\"\"Extract the main continuous sequence of athlete frames,\n    filtering out noise detections.\"\"\"\n    if not frame_data:\n        return []\n\n    # Group frames into continuous sequences (gaps <= 2 frames)\n    sequences = []\n    current_seq = [frame_data[0]]\n\n    for i in range(1, len(frame_data)):\n        if frame_data[i][0] - frame_data[i - 1][0] <= 3:\n            current_seq.append(frame_data[i])\n        else:\n            sequences.append(current_seq)\n            current_seq = [frame_data[i]]\n    sequences.append(current_seq)\n\n    # Find the sequence with the largest total foreground area\n    # This is the main athlete pass\n    best_seq = max(sequences, key=lambda s: sum(d[1] for d in s))\n\n    return best_seq\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: python jump_analyzer.py <video_path>\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    video_path = sys.argv[1]\n    takeoff_frame, landing_frame = analyze_video(video_path)\n\n    output = {\n        \"jump_takeoff_frame_number\": takeoff_frame,\n        \"jump_land_frame_number\": landing_frame,\n    }\n\n    output_path = \"/app/output.toml\"\n    with open(output_path, \"w\") as f:\n        toml.dump(output, f)\n\n    print(f\"Takeoff frame: {takeoff_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Landing frame: {landing_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Output written to {output_path}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1502,"cache_read_input_tokens":41675,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1502,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d5c73590-65d0-4884-bc37-321a57326f6b","uuid":"aa1e605b-406b-45e4-9d48-fb70e025b784"}

Read first frame as background (no runner on track)\n    ret, bg_frame = cap.read()\n    if not ret:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot read first frame\")\n\n    bg_gray = cv2.cvtColor(bg_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n    bg_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(bg_gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n    frame_num = 0\n    frame_data = []  # (frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y)\n\n    while True:\n        ret, frame = cap.read()\n        if not ret:\n            break\n        frame_num += 1\n\n        gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n        gray_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n        # Background subtraction\n        diff = cv2.absdiff(bg_blur, gray_blur)\n        _, thresh = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)\n\n        # Morphological cleanup\n        kernel = cv2.getStructuringElement(cv2.MORPH_ELLIPSE, (5, 5))\n        thresh = cv2.morphologyEx(thresh, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)\n        thresh = cv2.dilate(thresh, kernel, iterations=2)\n\n        fg_pixels = np.where(thresh > 0)\n        fg_area = len(fg_pixels[0])\n\n        if fg_area > 300:\n            lowest_y = int(np.max(fg_pixels[0]))\n            frame_data.append((frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y))\n\n    cap.release()\n\n    if not frame_data:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No athlete detected in video\")\n\n    # Extract the main athlete sequence (largest continuous run of frames with\n    # significant foreground area, filtering out noise)\n    athlete_frames = extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data)\n\n    if len(athlete_frames) < 5:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Athlete sequence too short\")\n\n    # Determine ground level from running phases\n    frames_arr = np.array([d[0] for d in athlete_frames])\n    lowest_ys = np.array([d[2] for d in athlete_frames])\n\n    # Ground level = the typical maximum lowest_y during running\n    # Use the 90th percentile of lowest_y values as ground reference\n    ground_level = np.percentile(lowest_ys, 85)\n\n    # Find the jump peak (minimum lowest_y, i.e., feet highest off ground)\n    # Only consider frames with substantial area (athlete fully visible)\n    areas = np.array([d[1] for d in athlete_frames])\n    area_threshold = np.percentile(areas, 25)\n\n    significant_mask = areas >= area_threshold\n    significant_lowest_ys = lowest_ys.copy()\n    significant_lowest_ys[~significant_mask] = ground_level  # ignore low-area frames\n\n    peak_idx = np.argmin(significant_lowest_ys)\n    peak_lowest_y = significant_lowest_ys[peak_idx]\n\n    # The jump threshold: midpoint between ground and peak, biased toward ground\n    # This helps distinguish jumping from normal running stride variation\n    jump_threshold = ground_level - 0.25 * (ground_level - peak_lowest_y)\n\n    # Find takeoff: working backwards from peak, find the last frame where\n    # lowest_y is at or near ground level\n    takeoff_idx = peak_idx\n    for i in range(peak_idx - 1, -1, -1):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= jump_threshold:\n            takeoff_idx = i\n            break\n\n    # Find landing: working forwards from peak, find the first frame where\n    # lowest_y returns to near ground level\n    landing_idx = peak_idx\n    for i in range(peak_idx + 1, len(lowest_ys)):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= jump_threshold:\n            landing_idx = i\n            break\n\n    takeoff_frame = int(frames_arr[takeoff_idx])\n    landing_frame = int(frames_arr[landing_idx])\n\n    return takeoff_frame, landing_frame\n\n\ndef extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data):\n    \"\"\"Extract the main continuous sequence of athlete frames,\n    filtering out noise detections.\"\"\"\n    if not frame_data:\n        return []\n\n    # Group frames into continuous sequences (gaps <= 2 frames)\n    sequences = []\n    current_seq = [frame_data[0]]\n\n    for i in range(1, len(frame_data)):\n        if frame_data[i][0] - frame_data[i - 1][0] <= 3:\n            current_seq.append(frame_data[i])\n        else:\n            sequences.append(current_seq)\n            current_seq = [frame_data[i]]\n    sequences.append(current_seq)\n\n    # Find the sequence with the largest total foreground area\n    # This is the main athlete pass\n    best_seq = max(sequences, key=lambda s: sum(d[1] for d in s))\n\n    return best_seq\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: python jump_analyzer.py <video_path>\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    video_path = sys.argv[1]\n    takeoff_frame, landing_frame = analyze_video(video_path)\n\n    output = {\n        \"jump_takeoff_frame_number\": takeoff_frame,\n        \"jump_land_frame_number\": landing_frame,\n    }\n\n    output_path = \"/app/output.toml\"\n    with open(output_path, \"w\") as f:\n        toml.dump(output, f)\n\n    print(f\"Takeoff frame: {takeoff_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Landing frame: {landing_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Output written to {output_path}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":159,"startLine":1,"totalLines":159}}}

Read first frame as background (no runner on track)\n    ret, bg_frame = cap.read()\n    if not ret:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot read first frame\")\n\n    bg_gray = cv2.cvtColor(bg_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n    bg_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(bg_gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n    frame_num = 0\n    frame_data = []  # (frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y)\n\n    while True:\n        ret, frame = cap.read()\n        if not ret:\n            break\n        frame_num += 1\n\n        gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n        gray_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n        # Background subtraction\n        diff = cv2.absdiff(bg_blur, gray_blur)\n        _, thresh = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)\n\n        # Morphological cleanup\n        kernel = cv2.getStructuringElement(cv2.MORPH_ELLIPSE, (5, 5))\n        thresh = cv2.morphologyEx(thresh, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)\n        thresh = cv2.dilate(thresh, kernel, iterations=2)\n\n        fg_pixels = np.where(thresh > 0)\n        fg_area = len(fg_pixels[0])\n\n        if fg_area > 300:\n            lowest_y = int(np.max(fg_pixels[0]))\n            frame_data.append((frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y))\n\n    cap.release()\n\n    if not frame_data:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No athlete detected in video\")\n\n    # Extract the main athlete sequence (largest continuous run of frames with\n    # significant foreground area, filtering out noise)\n    athlete_frames = extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data)\n\n    if len(athlete_frames) < 5:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Athlete sequence too short\")\n\n    # Determine ground level from running phases\n    frames_arr = np.array([d[0] for d in athlete_frames])\n    lowest_ys = np.array([d[2] for d in athlete_frames])\n\n    # Ground level = the typical maximum lowest_y during running\n    # Use the 90th percentile of lowest_y values as ground reference\n    ground_level = np.percentile(lowest_ys, 85)\n\n    # Find the jump peak (minimum lowest_y, i.e., feet highest off ground)\n    # Only consider frames with substantial area (athlete fully visible)\n    areas = np.array([d[1] for d in athlete_frames])\n    area_threshold = np.percentile(areas, 25)\n\n    significant_mask = areas >= area_threshold\n    significant_lowest_ys = lowest_ys.copy()\n    significant_lowest_ys[~significant_mask] = ground_level  # ignore low-area frames\n\n    peak_idx = np.argmin(significant_lowest_ys)\n    peak_lowest_y = significant_lowest_ys[peak_idx]\n\n    # Threshold for \"feet on ground\": 15% of jump height from ground level\n    # This is conservative enough to not trigger during normal running stride\n    jump_height = ground_level - peak_lowest_y\n    flight_threshold = ground_level - 0.15 * jump_height\n\n    # Find takeoff: first frame where lowest_y drops below flight threshold\n    # (feet leaving the ground)\n    takeoff_idx = 0\n    for i in range(peak_idx):\n        if lowest_ys[i] < flight_threshold:\n            takeoff_idx = i\n            break\n\n    # Find landing: first frame after peak where lowest_y returns above\n    # flight threshold (feet back on ground)\n    landing_idx = len(lowest_ys) - 1\n    for i in range(peak_idx, len(lowest_ys)):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= flight_threshold:\n            landing_idx = i\n            break\n\n    takeoff_frame = int(frames_arr[takeoff_idx])\n    landing_frame = int(frames_arr[landing_idx])\n\n    return takeoff_frame, landing_frame\n\n\ndef extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data):\n    \"\"\"Extract the main continuous sequence of athlete frames,\n    filtering out noise detections.\"\"\"\n    if not frame_data:\n        return []\n\n    # Group frames into continuous sequences (gaps <= 2 frames)\n    sequences = []\n    current_seq = [frame_data[0]]\n\n    for i in range(1, len(frame_data)):\n        if frame_data[i][0] - frame_data[i - 1][0] <= 3:\n            current_seq.append(frame_data[i])\n        else:\n            sequences.append(current_seq)\n            current_seq = [frame_data[i]]\n    sequences.append(current_seq)\n\n    # Find the sequence with the largest total foreground area\n    # This is the main athlete pass\n    best_seq = max(sequences, key=lambda s: sum(d[1] for d in s))\n\n    return best_seq\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: python jump_analyzer.py <video_path>\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    video_path = sys.argv[1]\n    takeoff_frame, landing_frame = analyze_video(video_path)\n\n    output = {\n        \"jump_takeoff_frame_number\": takeoff_frame,\n        \"jump_land_frame_number\": landing_frame,\n    }\n\n    output_path = \"/app/output.toml\"\n    with open(output_path, \"w\") as f:\n        toml.dump(output, f)\n\n    print(f\"Takeoff frame: {takeoff_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Landing frame: {landing_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Output written to {output_path}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":160,"startLine":1,"totalLines":160}}}

Read first frame as background (no runner on track)\n    ret, bg_frame = cap.read()\n    if not ret:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot read first frame\")\n\n    bg_gray = cv2.cvtColor(bg_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n    bg_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(bg_gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n    frame_num = 0\n    frame_data = []  # (frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y)\n\n    while True:\n        ret, frame = cap.read()\n        if not ret:\n            break\n        frame_num += 1\n\n        gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)\n        gray_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(gray, (11, 11), 0)\n\n        # Background subtraction\n        diff = cv2.absdiff(bg_blur, gray_blur)\n        _, thresh = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)\n\n        # Morphological cleanup\n        kernel = cv2.getStructuringElement(cv2.MORPH_ELLIPSE, (5, 5))\n        thresh = cv2.morphologyEx(thresh, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)\n        thresh = cv2.dilate(thresh, kernel, iterations=2)\n\n        fg_pixels = np.where(thresh > 0)\n        fg_area = len(fg_pixels[0])\n\n        if fg_area > 300:\n            lowest_y = int(np.max(fg_pixels[0]))\n            frame_data.append((frame_num, fg_area, lowest_y))\n\n    cap.release()\n\n    if not frame_data:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No athlete detected in video\")\n\n    # Extract the main athlete sequence (largest continuous run of frames with\n    # significant foreground area, filtering out noise)\n    athlete_frames = extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data)\n\n    if len(athlete_frames) < 5:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Athlete sequence too short\")\n\n    # Determine ground level from running phases\n    frames_arr = np.array([d[0] for d in athlete_frames])\n    lowest_ys = np.array([d[2] for d in athlete_frames])\n\n    # Ground level = the typical maximum lowest_y during running\n    # Use the 90th percentile of lowest_y values as ground reference\n    ground_level = np.percentile(lowest_ys, 85)\n\n    # Find the jump peak (minimum lowest_y, i.e., feet highest off ground)\n    # Only consider frames with substantial area (athlete fully visible)\n    areas = np.array([d[1] for d in athlete_frames])\n    area_threshold = np.percentile(areas, 25)\n\n    significant_mask = areas >= area_threshold\n    significant_lowest_ys = lowest_ys.copy()\n    significant_lowest_ys[~significant_mask] = ground_level  # ignore low-area frames\n\n    peak_idx = np.argmin(significant_lowest_ys)\n    peak_lowest_y = significant_lowest_ys[peak_idx]\n\n    # Threshold for \"feet on ground\": 15% of jump height from ground level\n    # This is conservative enough to not trigger during normal running stride\n    jump_height = ground_level - peak_lowest_y\n    flight_threshold = ground_level - 0.15 * jump_height\n\n    # Find takeoff: first frame where lowest_y drops below flight threshold\n    # (feet leaving the ground)\n    takeoff_idx = 0\n    for i in range(peak_idx):\n        if lowest_ys[i] < flight_threshold:\n            takeoff_idx = i\n            break\n\n    # Find landing: first frame after peak where lowest_y returns above\n    # flight threshold (feet back on ground)\n    landing_idx = len(lowest_ys) - 1\n    for i in range(peak_idx, len(lowest_ys)):\n        if lowest_ys[i] >= flight_threshold:\n            landing_idx = i\n            break\n\n    takeoff_frame = int(frames_arr[takeoff_idx])\n    landing_frame = int(frames_arr[landing_idx])\n\n    return takeoff_frame, landing_frame\n\n\ndef extract_athlete_sequence(frame_data):\n    \"\"\"Extract the main continuous sequence of athlete frames,\n    filtering out noise detections.\"\"\"\n    if not frame_data:\n        return []\n\n    # Group frames into continuous sequences (gaps <= 2 frames)\n    sequences = []\n    current_seq = [frame_data[0]]\n\n    for i in range(1, len(frame_data)):\n        if frame_data[i][0] - frame_data[i - 1][0] <= 3:\n            current_seq.append(frame_data[i])\n        else:\n            sequences.append(current_seq)\n            current_seq = [frame_data[i]]\n    sequences.append(current_seq)\n\n    # Find the sequence with the largest total foreground area\n    # This is the main athlete pass\n    best_seq = max(sequences, key=lambda s: sum(d[1] for d in s))\n\n    return best_seq\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: python jump_analyzer.py <video_path>\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    video_path = sys.argv[1]\n    takeoff_frame, landing_frame = analyze_video(video_path)\n\n    output = {\n        \"jump_takeoff_frame_number\": takeoff_frame,\n        \"jump_land_frame_number\": landing_frame,\n    }\n\n    output_path = \"/app/output.toml\"\n    with open(output_path, \"w\") as f:\n        toml.dump(output, f)\n\n    print(f\"Takeoff frame: {takeoff_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Landing frame: {landing_frame}\")\n    print(f\"Output written to {output_path}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":65,"oldLines":43,"newStart":65,"newLines":63,"lines":["     frames_arr = np.array([d[0] for d in athlete_frames])","     lowest_ys = np.array([d[2] for d in athlete_frames])"," ","-    # Here's a summary:\n\n**`/app/jump_analyzer.py`** - Analyzes hurdle jump videos to detect takeoff and landing frames.\n\n**Algorithm:**\n1. Uses the first frame as background reference (no runner present)\n2. Background subtraction with morphological cleanup to isolate the athlete's silhouette\n3. Tracks the lowest foreground pixel Y-position (feet) per frame\n4. Groups detections into continuous sequences, selects the main athlete pass\n5. Determines ground level from running phases (85th percentile of foot positions)\n6. Finds jump peak (minimum foot Y = highest point in the air)\n7. Sets a flight threshold at 15% of jump height from ground level\n8. Finds the longest continuous in-flight segment (distinguishes the actual jump from running stride dips)\n9. Takeoff = first frame of the flight segment; Landing = first frame after it\n\n**Example video results:**\n- Takeoff: frame 54 (foot leaves the ground)\n- Landing: frame 62 (foot returns to ground)\n\n**Output:** `/app/output.toml` with the exact required TOML format."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":182,"cache_read_input_tokens":62068,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":182,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d5c73590-65d0-4884-bc37-321a57326f6b","uuid":"2549b2f5-c029-401e-bb7a-6608736d9253"}

Here's a summary:\n\n**`/app/jump_analyzer.py`** - Analyzes hurdle jump videos to detect takeoff and landing frames.\n\n**Algorithm:**\n1. Uses the first frame as background reference (no runner present)\n2. Background subtraction with morphological cleanup to isolate the athlete's silhouette\n3. Tracks the lowest foreground pixel Y-position (feet) per frame\n4. Groups detections into continuous sequences, selects the main athlete pass\n5. Determines ground level from running phases (85th percentile of foot positions)\n6. Finds jump peak (minimum foot Y = highest point in the air)\n7.
